# Research — 2026-05-30

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents)

> Continuation of 2026-05-29. The 05-29 pass identified five concrete items
> (`/goal`, per-execute usage reset, global AGENTS.md, enforce skill
> `disabledTools`, `getProviderForModel` fallback, symlink-aware skills,
> `/code-review`). All seven now appear closed in `src/`. This pass integrates
> claude-code **v2.1.157 + v2.1.158** (May 28–30), kilocode **CLI 7.3.16–7.3.18**
> (May 29), and cline **3.0.14–3.0.15** (May 27–29).

## Executive Summary

- **All 7 carry-overs from 2026-05-29 are closed**: `/goal` (`src/command/goal.ts`),
  per-`execute()` usage isolation (each `agenticChat()` builds its own `totalUsage`
  at `src/core/agenticChat.ts:483`), global AGENTS.md (`src/agent/system.ts:160-187`,
  search order `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` → `~/.config` → `~`), `disabledTools` enforcement
  for active skills (`src/core/agenticChat.ts:381-385`), `fallbackModel` honored at
  the provider boundary (`src/providers/index.ts:86-141`), symlink-aware skill
  discovery with `realpathSync`/cycle protection (`src/skill/index.ts:218-253`),
  and `/code-review` with `--fix` and `--comment` (`src/command/codeReview.ts`,
  `codeReview.fix.test.ts`, `codeReview.comment.test.ts`).
- **claude-code v2.1.157 (May 30)** ships *plugin auto-load from `.claude/skills/`*
  (no marketplace required) and `claude plugin init <name>` to scaffold a new
  plugin in-place. Our plugin loader (`src/plugin/index.ts`) has neither — installed
  plugins must come from a registry/path. This is the highest-leverage parity item
  in the queue: it lowers the friction for one-off project plugins from "ship to a
  marketplace" to "drop a markdown file."
- **claude-code v2.1.157** also adds `tool_decision` telemetry events with
  `tool_parameters` (bash command, MCP/skill name) when `OTEL_LOG_TOOL_DETAILS=1`.
  We have no OTEL instrumentation in `src/`. Defer.
- **claude-code v2.1.157**: `claude agents` now honors the `agent` field in
  `settings.json` for dispatched sessions, with `--agent <name>` to override. We
  load custom agents (`src/agent/customAgentLoader.ts`) but have no per-session
  default-agent setting persisted across CLI invocations.
- **claude-code v2.1.158 (May 30)**: Auto-mode now available on Bedrock/Vertex/
  Foundry behind `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1`. Out of envelope (we route
  through SAP AI Core), but worth tracking when SAP exposes the equivalent
  classifier endpoint.
- **kilocode CLI 7.3.16–7.3.18** (May 29): the `read` tool now extracts text
  from **`.docx`**, **`.xlsx`**, and **`.ipynb`** files. Our `read` tool
  (`src/tool/tools/read.ts`) handles plain text + binary detection only; the
  separate `notebook` tool (`src/tool/tools/notebook.ts`) reads `.ipynb` cells
  but is not auto-dispatched from `read`. We have no DOCX/XLSX path at all.
  Closing this is small (mature `mammoth` and `xlsx` packages) and lifts
  alexi from "code-only reader" to "office-document reader."
- **cline #11103/#11113 (May 28–29) — already closed**: global AGENTS.md and
  symlinked-skill discovery both shipped on our side.
- **cline #11139 (May 29) — clickable OAuth URLs in TUI**: cline wraps OAuth
  URLs with OSC-8 hyperlink escapes so terminals that support them open the
  link directly. Our TUI (`src/cli/tui/components/`) emits no OSC-8 sequences
  anywhere. A small `linkify(url, label)` helper used in any auth/share
  message would match. Trivial — under 50 LOC.
- **kilocode #10707 docs PR (May 29)** clarifies that *per-directory AGENTS.md
  is loaded **dynamically** during file access, not statically at startup*.
  When the agent reads a file in a subdir, the loader walks upward and injects
  nearby `AGENTS.md` content as a `<system-reminder>`. This is a real
  capability gap for monorepos: today we load only the project root and global
  AGENTS.md once per session.
- **claude-code v2.1.152 — `MessageDisplay` and `SessionStart`'s
  `reloadSkills: true`/`hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle` outputs**: still
  unimplemented in our `src/hooks/index.ts` (events: `SessionStart`,
  `SessionEnd`, `PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, `PostToolUseFailure`,
  `PermissionRequest`, `Stop`, `Error`). No `MessageDisplay`, and `SessionStart`
  return values are not consumed beyond success/error. Defer `MessageDisplay`
  (no demand); add `reloadSkills` because we don't yet have **any** way to
  re-scan skills mid-session — every skill change today requires a restart.

## Detailed Findings

### What was closed since 2026-05-29 (verified in code)
- `/goal` autonomous loop — `src/command/goal.ts:71-181` with goal.progress events
  and an LLM-based condition evaluator.
- Per-`execute()` usage isolation — `src/core/agenticChat.ts:483-488` allocates a
  fresh `totalUsage` per call; `src/core/__tests__/agenticChat.test.ts:258`
  exercises accumulation-within-iteration only.
- Global AGENTS.md resolution — `src/agent/system.ts:160-187` with the exact
  $XDG_CONFIG_HOME → ~/.config → ~ search order and resolved-path de-dup against
  project AGENTS.md.
- Skill `disabledTools` enforcement — `src/core/agenticChat.ts:381-385` filters
  tools when `options?.activeSkill?.disabledTools` is set;
  `agenticChat.test.ts:552` covers the active-skill case.
- `getProviderForModel` fallback — `src/providers/index.ts:86-141` resolves
  `routingConfig.preferences.fallbackModel` and is called from
  `streamingOrchestrator.ts:120`.
- Symlink-aware skill discovery — `src/skill/index.ts:223-253` (`realpathSync`,
  `seenResolved` set, graceful failure).
- `/code-review` command + `--fix` + `--comment` — `src/command/codeReview.ts`
  with split tests for the two flag paths.

### Still open from 2026-05-29
- None.

### New gaps (sorted by leverage)

#### 1. Per-directory AGENTS.md injected as `<system-reminder>` during file reads (Category: tool — medium, 1 day)
- **What**: When the `read` tool (or `multiedit`/`edit`/`grep` `--head-limit`
  paths) accesses a file outside the project root, walk upward from the file's
  directory toward the workspace root collecting any `AGENTS.md` that hasn't
  already been included in this session. Inject each as a `<system-reminder>`
  block in the next assistant turn (or as a tool-result preface). Cache by
  resolved path so the same subdir AGENTS isn't re-injected on every read.
- **Why**: kilocode #10707 (May 29) documents this as the canonical "per-dir
  AGENTS.md" UX, and it's the missing half of the global AGENTS.md story we
  shipped this week. In monorepos, `apps/api/AGENTS.md` and
  `packages/db/AGENTS.md` carry rules that the project root AGENTS.md cannot
  encode without bloat. Today our agent only ever sees the workspace root +
  global file.
- **Source**: kilocode#10707 + per-directory AGENTS.md design notes
  (`src/session/instruction.ts` `_resolve()` upstream).
- **Size**: medium (1 day) — modest because `loadInstructionFiles()`
  (`src/agent/system.ts:135+`) already centralizes file loading; the new
  surface is per-tool-call rather than per-session.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - Add `instructionsForPath(absPath: string, workdir: string, seen: Set<string>): string[]`
    in `src/agent/system.ts`. Walk `path.dirname(absPath)` up to `workdir`,
    look for `AGENTS.md` at each step, return contents in nearest-first order.
  - In `src/tool/tools/read.ts` and the bulk-tool entry points, after a successful
    read, append any newly discovered AGENTS.md content to `ToolResult.metadata.systemReminders`.
  - `streamingOrchestrator.ts` flushes pending `systemReminders` into the next user message
    as `<system-reminder>...</system-reminder>`.

#### 2. DOCX + XLSX text extraction in the `read` tool (Category: tool — small, < 4h)
- **What**: When `read` is called on a `.docx`, `.xlsx`, or `.xlsm` file,
  extract text via `mammoth` (DOCX → markdown) and `xlsx` (XLSX → CSV per
  sheet, headers + first-N-rows preview, with sheet names listed). Bound the
  output by the same line-limit rules already in `read`. Auto-dispatch from
  the existing `read` tool — do not make users guess a separate command.
- **Why**: kilocode CLI 7.3.16–7.3.18 (May 29) shipped exactly this pair plus
  `.ipynb` (we already have `notebook` for that, but it's not auto-dispatched
  from `read` either). The friction today is real: any office artifact in the
  repo (RFCs, design docs, dependency spreadsheets, change logs in `.docx`)
  silently fails or returns binary noise. `mammoth` (~70 KB gz) and `xlsx`
  (already vendored by many projects) are mature.
- **Source**: kilocode#10737 (`feat(cli): support DOCX text extraction in read tool`,
  May 29), kilocode#10740 (`feat(cli): support XLSX text extraction in read tool`,
  May 29), kilocode#10733 (`feat(cli): read Jupyter notebooks as cell content`,
  May 29).
- **Size**: small (< 4h). One PR for the dispatcher table inside `read.ts` +
  two extractor functions; the existing line-limit and binary-detection paths
  cover the rest.
- **Note on size bounding**: kilocode also shipped `fix(cli): bound XLSX read
  input size` (May 29) — copy that early, an unbounded XLSX with hidden styles
  can balloon. Refuse files > 25 MB and rows > 5,000 with a clear "use a
  preview" message.

#### 3. Plugin auto-load from `.alexi/skills/` + `alexi plugin init` scaffolder (Category: plugin — medium, 1-2 days)
- **What**: Two changes:
  - **Auto-load**: `src/plugin/index.ts` should treat any directory under
    `.alexi/skills/` (project) and `~/.alexi/skills/` (global) containing a
    `plugin.json` as an installed plugin, even if it isn't in the plugin
    registry. Today plugins must be added through the plugin loader's
    explicit registration paths.
  - **Scaffolder**: add `alexi plugin init <name>` (alias under
    `src/cli/commands/`) that creates `.alexi/skills/<name>/` with a minimal
    `plugin.json`, a sample `commands/<name>.md`, and a README. Mirrors
    `claude plugin init <name>`.
- **Why**: claude-code v2.1.157 (May 30) made this the default authoring loop
  precisely because the marketplace round-trip (publish → install → version
  bump) is overkill for project-local extensions. With the rule-contributions
  pattern from cline #11127 (May 29), plugins are turning into the standard
  vehicle for opinionated team rules. Lowering the on-ramp matters.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.157 (May 30) — "Plugins in
  `.claude/skills` directories are now automatically loaded, no marketplace
  required" + "Added `claude plugin init <name>` to scaffold a new plugin in
  `.claude/skills`".
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days). Split as auto-load (small) + scaffolder
  (small) PRs to keep diffs reviewable. The auto-load PR needs the same
  symlink-resolved de-dup pattern we already use in `skillDirectories()`.

#### 4. Default-agent setting honored across sessions (`agent` in user config) (Category: config — small, < 4h)
- **What**: Add an `agent` field at the top level of the user config schema
  (`src/config/userConfig.ts`) and read it in `src/cli/commands/agent.ts` /
  `chat.ts` so the value is the default for new sessions. CLI flag
  `--agent <name>` overrides for a single invocation. Existing
  `customAgentLoader.ts` already supplies the agent registry; this is
  wiring + a one-line schema addition.
- **Why**: claude-code v2.1.157 added exactly this for `claude agents` — many
  users want a single per-project default ("debug" for a flaky monorepo,
  "explore" for a research repo) and don't want to type it every time. Our
  custom-agent infra already exists; the missing piece is "where do you
  declare which one is default?"
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.157 — "`claude agents`: the `agent`
  field in `settings.json` is now honored for dispatched sessions, with
  `--agent <name>` to override it".
- **Size**: small (< 4h). Mostly schema + dispatch site changes; one new test
  in `src/agent/__tests__/`.

#### 5. OSC-8 clickable URLs in the TUI (Category: tool — small, < 2h) — *cosmetic but high-visibility*
- **What**: Add a `hyperlink(url, label?)` helper in `src/cli/tui/utils/`
  that emits `\x1b]8;;<url>\x07<label>\x1b]8;;\x07` and use it in the
  share-link, OAuth-URL, and `/help` link sites in the TUI. Detect terminal
  support via `supports-hyperlinks` (already a transitive dep of many ink
  helpers) and gracefully fall back to plain text otherwise.
- **Why**: cline#11139 (May 29) shipped this as a one-off for OAuth URLs;
  the same wrapper applies anywhere we print a URL. No-op cost in
  unsupporting terminals; large UX win in iTerm2/WezTerm/kitty/Ghostty/Apple
  Terminal.app/VS Code Terminal/Cursor/Windsurf. Trivial enough that it
  pairs well with another small PR in the cycle.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11139 (May 29).
- **Size**: small (< 2h).

#### 6. `SessionStart` hook can return `reloadSkills: true` + new `/reload-skills` command (Category: command — small, < 4h)
- **What**: Two coupled changes:
  - Extend `HookResult` (`src/hooks/index.ts:76`) with an optional
    `hookSpecificOutput?.reloadSkills?: boolean`. When the hook manager sees
    this on a `SessionStart` result, call into the skill loader's existing
    `loadSkillsFromDirectory()` re-scan path and replace the active skill
    set.
  - Add a `/reload-skills` command (`src/command/`) that does the same thing
    interactively. No arguments.
- **Why**: claude-code v2.1.152 — and reaffirmed in the v2.1.157 plugin auto-
  load story — turns "install a skill mid-session" into a normal flow. With
  our auto-load + scaffolder above (item 3), users *expect* a way to refresh
  without restarting. Today there is none.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.152 ("`/reload-skills`" + "`SessionStart`
  hooks can now return `reloadSkills: true`").
- **Size**: small (< 4h).

### Notable upstream items deliberately deferred
- **Auto-mode on Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry (v2.1.158)**: SAP-AI-Core does not yet
  expose an equivalent classifier deployment. Re-evaluate when it does. No
  action this cycle.
- **`tool_decision`/`tool_parameters` OTEL events (v2.1.157)**: we have no
  OTEL instrumentation. Adding it would be a full feature on its own; defer
  until there's a downstream consumer (otelcol pipeline, prometheus, etc.).
- **`MessageDisplay` hook (v2.1.152)**: still no use case. Defer.
- **`claude agents` PR column / `! <cmd>` background sessions (v2.1.153–157)**:
  these depend on a background-agent infrastructure we don't have. Out of
  envelope.
- **Dynamic workflows + Opus 4.8 default-high-effort (v2.1.154+)**: upstream
  product orchestration; not a CLI parity item for us.
- **Plugin marketplace `defaultEnabled: false` (v2.1.154)**: only relevant
  once we publish a marketplace. Our loader uses on-demand activation; no
  user-facing problem yet.
- **`skipLfs` for plugin marketplace sources (v2.1.153)**: depends on a Git
  marketplace. Defer.
- **kilocode Effect refactors (#10727, #10731 etc., May 29)**: internal-only
  on their side. No action.
- **cline Discord-connector work (#11114, #11115, #11121, #11126)**: out of
  envelope for our SAP-AI-Core CLI.
- **POSIX `cd`/`pushd`/subshell permission audit (deferred from v2.1.149
  PowerShell finding on 2026-05-29)**: still on the to-do list. Open a
  tracking issue if not already created.

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Ship the small wins as one cycle (~1 day total)**:
   - Item #2: DOCX + XLSX in `read` — biggest user-visible capability gain.
   - Item #5: OSC-8 clickable URLs — trivial, high signal.
   - Item #4: default-agent in user config — schema + wiring.
   - Item #6: `reloadSkills` hook output + `/reload-skills` command.
   These four can land as four separate PRs over a single day; they touch
   different files (`src/tool/tools/read.ts`, `src/cli/tui/utils/`,
   `src/config/userConfig.ts`, `src/hooks/index.ts` + `src/command/`) and
   have no inter-dependencies.
2. **Item #3 (plugin auto-load + `alexi plugin init` scaffolder)** is the
   medium-effort feature for this cycle. Two sub-PRs:
   - (a) Auto-load `.alexi/skills/<name>/plugin.json` with `realpathSync` de-dup,
   - (b) `alexi plugin init <name>` scaffolder mirroring claude-code's UX.
   Item #6 (`/reload-skills`) makes (a) usable mid-session — sequence them.
3. **Item #1 (per-directory AGENTS.md as `<system-reminder>`)** is the
   highest-value behaviour change. Specify it as a separate plan: it
   touches the tool result type, the streaming orchestrator's
   user-message-prefacing path, and the agent system instructions. Plan it
   in the next cycle, ship in the cycle after.
4. **Open the deferred POSIX `cd`/`pushd` permission audit issue** if it
   wasn't filed last cycle — it's not getting smaller.
5. **Tracker hygiene**: the 2026-05-29 entry's "5 open items" is now zero.
   Reflect the win in the next planning doc so the velocity signal isn't lost.
6. **Re-evaluate next cycle**: SAP-AI-Core auto-mode classifier availability,
   OTEL instrumentation demand, dynamic-workflows feasibility under
   single-binary CLI constraints.
